# adagraph

Design a two-stage adaptive trial using a graph-based closed-testing
sequence, while controlling the FWER using the conditional error rate
method.

## Installation

You can install the development version of adagraph from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r

# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("nebtu/adagraph")
```

## Example

``` r

library(adagraph)

alpha_spending_f <- function(x, t) 2 - 2 * pnorm(qnorm(1 - x / 2) / sqrt(t))
design <- cer_design(
  correlation = rbind(H1 = c(1, NA), H2 = c(NA, 1)),
  weights = c(2 / 3, 1 / 3),
  alpha = 0.05,
  test_m = rbind(c(0, 1), c(1, 0)),
  alpha_spending_f = alpha_spending_f,
  t = 0.5
)


design <- cer_interim_test(design, c(0.001, 0.02)) |>
  cer_drop_hypotheses(1) |>
  cer_final_test(c(NA, 0.01))

design
#> A CER Design object, for testing 2 hypotheses at FWER 0.05.
#> 
#> ── An interim test has been performed. ─────────────────────────────────────────
#> Hypotheses rejected at the interim: H1
#> ── The following characteristics have been adapted: ────────────────────────────
#> • Hypotheses weights
#> • Graph Transition Matrix
#> ── Final test result ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#> Hypotheses rejected: H1 and H2
```

# Package index

## All functions

- [`adagraph_design()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/adagraph_design.md)
  : Make a new (generic) trial design
- [`cer_adapt()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/cer_adapt.md)
  : Adapt the trial design after the interim test
- [`cer_adapt_bounds()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/cer_adapt_bounds.md)
  : Adjust bounds after changing some design parameters
- [`cer_alt_drop_hypotheses()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/cer_alt_drop_hypotheses.md)
  : Adapt a cer design by dropping hypotheses, with a simplified
  strategy for redistributing weights
- [`cer_design()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/cer_design.md)
  : Make a new CER trial design
- [`cer_drop_hypotheses()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/cer_drop_hypotheses.md)
  : Adapt a cer design by dropping hypotheses
- [`cer_final_test()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/cer_final_test.md)
  : Test a cer design for early rejection of hypotheses and calculate
  the CER for adaptations
- [`cer_interim_test()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/cer_interim_test.md)
  : Test a cer design for early rejection of hypotheses and calculate
  the CER for adaptations
- [`cer_prep_bounds()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/cer_prep_bounds.md)
  : Calculate bounds for preplanned test
- [`check_consonance()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/check_consonance.md)
  : Check if a design is consonant
- [`export_gmcp_lite()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/export_gmcp_lite.md)
  : Export the closed testing strategy as a gMCPLite graph object
- [`export_graphical_mcp()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/export_graphical_mcp.md)
  : Export the closed testing strategy as a graphicalMCP graph object
- [`get_cer()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/get_cer.md) :
  Get the Conditional Error Rate for a intersection of hypotheses
- [`get_data_gen()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/get_data_gen.md)
  : Get a data generating function for simulating a trial
- [`get_data_gen_2()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/get_data_gen_2.md)
  : Get a data generating function for simulating the second stage of a
  trial
- [`get_subgroup_correlation()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/get_subgroup_correlation.md)
  : Get correlation between different subgroups and arms
- [`get_t()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/get_t.md) : Get
  the time fractions for given sample sizes
- [`intersection_hypotheses()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/intersection_hypotheses.md)
  : Get information about the intersection hypothesis of a design
- [`multiarm_cer_design()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/multiarm_cer_design.md)
  : Make a new multiarm CER trial design
- [`multiarm_drop_arms()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/multiarm_drop_arms.md)
  : Drop arm from a multiarm design
- [`redistribute_n()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/redistribute_n.md)
  : Get the amount of people for each hypotheses in the 2. stage of a
  trial assuming a fixed population
- [`sim_trial()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/sim_trial.md)
  : Run a series of simulated trials according to a specified design
- [`trial_adapt_n()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/trial_adapt_n.md)
  : Adapt the patient numbers for the second stage of a trial design
- [`trial_design()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/trial_design.md)
  : Make a new trial design with multiple arms, endpoints or subgroups
- [`trial_drop_groups()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/trial_drop.md)
  [`trial_drop_arms()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/trial_drop.md)
  [`trial_drop_endpoints()`](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/reference/trial_drop.md)
  : Drop groups, arms or endpoints from a trial design

### All vignettes

- [Example for using the CER Method with
  adagraph](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/articles/example_cer_design.md):
- [Example for simulating a multiarm trial with
  adagraph](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/articles/example_simulation.md):
- [Example for using the CER Method for a trial design (with subgroups)
  with
  adagraph](https://nebtu.github.io/adagraph/articles/example_trial_design.md):
